Empires & Puzzles Wang Yuanji Review: The 4-Star Healer That Outclasses Legendaries
Is Wang Yuanji still the queen of 4-star healers? We revisit the War of Three Kingdoms classic to see if her massive overheal can still keep your team alive in 2026.
Wang Yuanji: The "Butterfly" Effect of Massive Healing
Wang Yuanji: The Gentle Noblewoman Who Heals Like a Goddess

Wang Yuanji arrived in Empires & Puzzles back in March 2022 as part of the War of Three Kingdoms event, and she immediately caused a stir. Why? Because she healed harder than almost every 5-star hero in the game at the time. A "Slow" Holy Sorcerer, she brought a mechanic that was rare for 4-stars: massive Boosted Health combined with a hard counter to Fiends.

. While her costume has since been released, the original Wang Yuanji remains a distinct entity because of one simple fact: her raw healing output is significantly higher without the fancy dress. She is the definition of a "stat stick" healer—she doesn't buff defense or attack, she just pumps an absurd amount of HP into your team.

Wang Yuanji's design is elegance personified. Dressed in white and gold with flowing fabrics that defy gravity, she looks every bit the "Gentle Noblewoman" her title suggests. The art captures a sense of serene power—she isn't armored for war; she's dressed for court, yet she wields magic that can undo the darkest curses. Compared to her costume, which leans into a more structured, yellow-dominant "Calligrapher" aesthetic, the base art feels lighter and more ethereal. It’s the calm before the massive HP spike.
Let's address the elephant in the room: her base stats are from 2022.

This is a flat value, not a percentage. For 4-star heroes with lower health pools, 625 HP is practically 40-50% of their max health. Even for 5-stars, it's a massive chunk.

This is still one of the highest raw healing numbers on a special skill in the 4-star tier.
By destroying Fiends instantly, she effectively "heals" the damage those Fiends would have dealt or absorbed. Against a hero like

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The War of Three Kingdoms heroes rely heavily on their family bonus to compete with newer stats.

The Kingdom family bonus is unique because it rewards you for using Minions. You gain mana generation and healing for each Minion you own. While Wang Yuanji doesn't summon minions herself, pairing her with heroes like
, or even generic minion summoners allows her to charge faster. It’s a synergy-dependent bonus that can push her from "too slow" to "just right."
fits her kit perfectly. Since her main job is to keep the team alive, boosting that output further ensures that when she fires, your team is back to full health (and then some).

Upon release, Wang Yuanji was hailed as a "5-star in disguise." Players realized that her 625 boosted health was superior to many legendary healers like Heimdall or Professor Lidenbrock in terms of raw numbers relative to her rarity.
Her stats have aged poorly. She is squishy. A strong breeze from a modern 5-star sniper like
will knock her out before she charges. However, her Special Skill has not been power-crept. 625 Boosted Health is
You cannot just throw her onto a defense team and expect her to survive. She shines in

wars/tournaments and in PvE (Towers, Events) where you can manage the board to charge her safely. She is still a top-tier pick for 4-star tournaments where healing is allowed, simply because few 4-stars can undo damage as effectively as she can.
While this review focuses on the base, it's worth noting the trade-off.

625 HP is a game-changer, effectively doubling the HP of 3-star and 4-star allies.

She doesn't hurt the enemy. If you are behind, she stalls the game but doesn't help you kill anyone.

Overheal is permanent in these modes. She can ramp your team to 200% HP in the first few waves, making the boss waves trivial.
The AI plays healers well enough, and the massive health buffer makes auto-farming safer.
Wang Yuanji - Liu Bei - Sun Quan - Diaochian - Cao Cao
Maximize the Kingdom family bonus. The minions from Liu Bei/Diaochan/Sun Quan feed Wang Yuanji mana and healing, mitigating her slow speed.
Wang Yuanji - Gullinbursti - Proteus - Wilbur - Wu Kong
The "Double Overheal" strategy. Wang Yuanji and Gullinbursti together make your team unkillable in difficult map stages or events.

If you pull Wang Yuanji early, you have won the lottery. She will carry you through Season 2, 3, and 4 on Hard mode. Prioritize leveling her immediately. She effectively gives your team 5-star durability. Do not worry about her slow speed in map stages; you can use mana potions.
She is a niche tool for you now. You likely have 5-star healers who do more. However, keep her for
(where survivability matters). If you have the Costume, level it for the stats, but consider using the Base form in events where you need raw HP more than defense.
When Wang Yuanji was released, the forums were ablaze.
"Why is this hero slow??? Unless there is fiends, she is way below average,"
But the sentiment quickly shifted as players realized the power of the overheal.
"I think making her faster than slow outclasses other slow boosters like gullinbursti... She already has more boosted health baseline than the boar."
One user summed it up perfectly:
"I look at her as a healer, fiend removal is a bonus. Her boost is one of the highest in the game."
History proved the optimists right. She became a staple in Rush wars, often frustrating attackers who couldn't break through the wall of HP.

Wang Yuanji is the "Gentle Noblewoman" who kills you with kindness—specifically, by making her allies so healthy that you break your sword trying to cut them. While her stats are showing their age, her special skill remains a benchmark for raw healing power. She is a timeless classic in the 4-star roster.
If you need raw HP and hate Fiends, the Base version is still the queen. Keep her, emblem her, and let her carry your team to immortality.
A comprehensive review of Wang Yuanji, the Holy 4-Star hero from the War of Three Kingdoms. We analyze her massive boosted health, stats, and viability in the modern meta.
